prose:
Method provenance (follows chemkit–CALC reporting standards)

• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor  
  – headline value reported here: maximum electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ = 2.1028319 (dimensionless) for atom 3 (index 2, C).  
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF.  
• Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied geometry “phenol.xyz” (no re-optimization).  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).  
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) states generated automatically for the N ± 1 calculations.  
• Software/backend: PySCF via chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill.  
• Convergence: SCF cycles for N, N + 1, and N − 1 states converged; integrity check status “ok”.  
• Warnings (verbatim from engine):
    – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – common header present  

Live log: the run was streamed to the engine-generated `.out` file in the working directory (path printed during execution) and is available for inspection.

Result highlights
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, 0-based index 2) with f⁺ = 2.1028319.  
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, 0-based index 0) with f⁻ = 0.16201477.
